title: Create M5 planning package
summary: Create the reviewed M5 alpha content framework planning package from the WM-0071 future M5 entry prompt, including scenario contract, ADR, task DAG, risk/performance/save/Worker/content-validation plan, and executable M5 task packets without implementing runtime M5 behavior.
acceptance: M5 planning package adopts the reviewed WM-0071 future M5 entry prompt and records startup audit evidence from main at M4 closeout

M5 scenario contract defines a concrete alpha content framework path with at least three anomaly rules, faction/governance hooks, season/event pool expectations, data-mod validation boundaries and explicit M6 non-goals

ADR-0010 or equivalent records M5 ownership boundaries for anomaly roster, faction/campaign facts, governance, season/event pools, content schemas, data-mod validation, save/replay scope, Worker projections and benchmark policy

M5 task DAG JSON packets are created with dependencies, milestone, owners, reviewers, allowed paths, forbidden paths, validation commands, risks, file ownership, benchmark impact, rollback model and owner gates

Planning report records M4 baseline evidence, M5 objective, protected M0-M4 regression scope, performance/invariant plan, save/Worker policy, data-mod policy, Spark classification and M6 readiness stop signs
No runtime M5 implementation begins in WM-0072 and no M6 task is created, promoted, claimed or implemented

history.details: Created M5 planning package: scenario contract, ADR-0010, roadmap plan, proposed WM-0073 through WM-0083 task DAG, reports, project-state marker, M5 performance/save/Worker/data-mod policy and M6 stop signs; required checks pass; no runtime M5 or M6 work started.

verified: No blocking findings. Confirmed WM-0072 adopts the reviewed WM-0071 future M5 prompt, preserves the M4 closeout baseline, limits edits to allowed planning/control-plane paths plus workflow inbox, and creates only proposed/unclaimed WM-0073 through WM-0083 M5 task packets. ADR-0010, scenario contract and roadmap plan cover anomaly roster, faction/governance facts, season/event pools, data-mod validation, focused save/replay, Worker projections, benchmark policy, owner-store boundaries and M6 stop signs. Required gates passed; ci:local used a temporary WM_ARTIFACT_DIR. Runtime M5 and M6 remain unstarted.

Reviewed M5 planning package accepted; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality. Downstream M5 packets remain proposed until WM-0072 is closed; no runtime M5 or M6 work started.

objective: Create the reviewed M5 planning package, including scenario contract, ADR, roadmap plan, proposed task DAG and reports, without implementing runtime M5 behavior or starting M6.
benchmarkImpact: None directly. WM-0072 defines required M5 benchmark and invariant tasks while preserving the existing 10 percent warning and 20 percent blocking thresholds.
reviewRouting: Systems-architect completes to reviewer. Project-director integrates only after independent verification that downstream M5 tasks remain proposed and no runtime M5 or M6 work started.
rollbackModel: Revert the M5 planning documents, ADR-0010, proposed WM-0073 through WM-0083 packets, WM-0072 reports and narrow roadmap/project-state markers; restore M4 closed / M5 unstarted.
ownerGate: Do not edit product runtime tests benchmarks package manifests or generated baselines in WM-0072
Keep all downstream M5 tasks proposed until WM-0072 is reviewed integrated and done
Define explicit block conditions for owner-store gaps global scans public protocol save schema dependencies UI authority content-schema drift and M6 scope creep
Preserve M0-M4 regression protection independent reviewer separation and benchmark thresholds
Do not create promote claim implement or review M6 work

sparkEligibility.reason: M5 milestone planning, ADR ownership, task DAG design, save/replay scope, content schema policy and benchmark policy cannot be assigned to Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Confirmed WM-0072 adopts the reviewed WM-0071 future M5 prompt, preserves the M4 closeout baseline, limits edits to allowed planning/control-plane paths plus workflow inbox, and creates only proposed/unclaimed WM-0073 through WM-0083 M5 task packets. ADR-0010, scenario contract and roadmap plan cover anomaly roster, faction/governance facts, season/event pools, data-mod validation, focused save/replay, Worker projections, benchmark policy, owner-store boundaries and M6 stop signs. Required gates passed; ci:local used a temporary WM_ARTIFACT_DIR. Runtime M5 and M6 remain unstarted.

integration.summary: Reviewed M5 planning package accepted; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality. Downstream M5 packets remain proposed until WM-0072 is closed; no runtime M5 or M6 work started.
